page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational and text processing capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests, as well as text generation for small language models. Multi-core performance, image processing, and cryptographic operations fall within the top 25% of tested servers. However, memory bandwidth tests and database operations show average results, and text generation for large language models (70B) is weak, representing a clear tradeoff for workloads requiring massive parameter processing without GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i-flex.8xlarge offers a high density of DDR5 memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The inclusion of Intel AMX instructions provides hardware-accelerated matrix operations, which benefits small-to-medium machine learning inference without the cost of a dedicated GPU. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, text processing pipelines, web serving, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, a 480 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, with no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core and text processing performance, alongside strong results in cryptography and small-model LLM inference. However, large-model text generation is weak, and memory bandwidth remains average. This resource profile makes the instance highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, web serving, and text processing pipelines that benefit from high memory density and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX without requiring local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.
